page: Amazon Web Services c8ine.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. Powered by an Intel processor running at a dedicated clock speed of 3.9 GHz, this instance features 4 virtual CPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated resource allocation and minimal virtualization overhead.

The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per physical core. It does not include local instance storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached storage. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. No graphics processing units are included in this configuration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8ine.xlarge offers a high-frequency compute profile relative to its memory footprint, making it suitable for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory allocations or local NVMe storage. Appropriate workloads for this instance type include batch processing, distributed analytics, high-performance web serving, and compute-intensive application servers.
description: Amazon Web Services c8ine.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel processor. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage. With a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps and no integrated GPUs, this instance is designed for workloads requiring high CPU clock speeds relative to memory capacity. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound tasks that do not demand massive memory footprints or local disk I/O. Best-fit workloads include batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 instance featuring high-frequency Intel processors and dedicated Nitro hypervisor resources for compute-intensive workloads.
